minimal_claim: Recent ZIFB studies demonstrate high-capacity appeal and improved electrodes, electrolytes, cell designs, rate performance, and cycling stability.
source_locator: Li2015 abstract/results; Xie2018 abstract; Weng2017 p.1; Fan2024 p.1; Wen2025 p.1; Huang2025 p.1; WuLoading2025 p.1
evidence_excerpt_le25_words: Weng: 'complexing agent to stabilize the iodine, forming iodine-bromide ions'.
assessment: Li2015, Xie2018, Weng2017 and Fan2024 concern flow batteries; Wen2025, Huang2025 and WuLoading2025 concern the broader/static Zn-I2 field. Calling the whole set ZIFB evidence conflates architectures.
safer_wording: Over the past decade, zinc-polyiodide flow batteries have combined high-energy iodide chemistry with electrolyte, membrane and cell-design strategies for improved power and cycling performance \\citep{Li2015,Xie2018,Weng2017,Fan2024}.

minimal_claim: During positive-electrode charge, iodide is oxidized to molecular iodine.
source_locator: Wen2025 pp.4-5, low-valence conversion discussion and Eqs. 1-4
evidence_excerpt_le25_words: During charging, I- is first oxidized to I2.
assessment: The review explicitly states the charge direction and the I-/I2/I3- sequence.
safer_wording: Retain; optionally write the half-reaction as an idealized bookkeeping reaction.

minimal_claim: Excess iodide binds aqueous I2 through I2 + I- <=> I3-, and I3- is the predominant soluble polyiodide in the stated treatment.
source_locator: Palmer1984 p.673 Eq.1 and pp.677-679 Tables III-IV; Wen2025 p.5 Eqs.2-3 and adjacent text
evidence_excerpt_le25_words: Palmer: 'equilibrium constant at 25 C is 698 +/- 10'.
assessment: Palmer directly establishes the aqueous triiodide formation equilibrium; Wen states that other polyiodides are less stable and commonly represented by I3-.
safer_wording: In excess iodide, aqueous I2 is partitioned by I2 + I- <=> I3-; I3- is commonly treated as the dominant soluble polyiodide \\citep{Palmer1984,Wen2025}.

minimal_claim: Iodide rapidly binds I2.
source_locator: Palmer1984 throughout is an equilibrium/activity study, not a kinetic study; Wen2025 p.4 says the reaction is favorable and rapid but is a review statement
evidence_excerpt_le25_words: Wen: 'this reaction is more favorable and occurs rapidly'.
assessment: The citation pair supports equilibrium/speciation, but Palmer does not measure a complexation rate. The kinetic adverb rests only on a secondary review and is unnecessary.
safer_wording: Remove 'rapidly': excess iodide binds aqueous I2 through the I2 + I- <=> I3- equilibrium.

minimal_claim: Nanoporous carbon hosts can retain substantial solid iodine while maintaining reversible electrochemistry.
source_locator: SolidI2_NatCommun2020 pp.4-6, Figs.2g,3c-d,4a-c; Fitzek2023 pp.6-9, Figs.2-4
evidence_excerpt_le25_words: There are no signs for performance degradation after cycling several hundred times.
assessment: Directly supported for nanoporous carbon: about 30% pore occupancy, near-100% CE over hundreds of cycles, and reversible spectroscopic/structural changes. It is host-specific, not transferable to felt morphology.
safer_wording: Retain with 'nanoporous carbon' explicit; the following non-transfer sentence is essential.

minimal_claim: A dense iodine film can cause a large increase in positive-electrode charge-transfer resistance under other conditions.
source_locator: Jang2021 p.6385 abstract; pp.6388-6391, Figs.3-6
evidence_excerpt_le25_words: dramatic 9-fold increase of Rct to 147.4 ohm cm2
assessment: Jang directly measured the porous-to-dense film transition, Rct increase from below 16.5 to 147.4 ohm cm2, and >300 mV overpotential rise above 400 mA cm-2.
safer_wording: Use 'dense iodine film' rather than the broader 'dense iodine deposits'.

minimal_claim: In another iodine cell, optical microscopy shows a charged-state iodine film and residual separated particles after discharge.
source_locator: LiDualPlate2025 pp.3163-3164, Fig.3a-b and Fig.S12
evidence_excerpt_le25_words: yellow iodine films immediately cover the carbon fiber; A few I2 particles are observed
assessment: Direct in-situ optical evidence on graphite-felt fibers: film coverage after 210 s charge and a few particles after 420 s discharge. The chemistry is a dual-plating G4/I- cell, not the present electrolyte.
safer_wording: Retain the explicit 'different iodine cell' boundary and do not transfer morphology thresholds.

minimal_claim: Continuum porous-electrode models calculate species transport, current distribution and potential fields across a flow cell.
source_locator: Newman1962 pp.1183-1191; Tichter2020 thesis; Chakrabarti2020 p.5433 abstract and sections 3.3-4; AparicioMauricio2023 pp.1-6, Eqs.2-31 and Figs.4-6
evidence_excerpt_le25_words: Aparicio: 'tertiary current distribution model' with concentration, current and potential fields.
assessment: Newman and Tichter are foundational porous/macroporous-electrode theory, not flow-cell demonstrations. Chakrabarti and Aparicio support the flow-battery extension. The grouped sentence obscures this distinction.
safer_wording: Porous-electrode theory resolves coupled current and species transport \\citep{Newman1962,Tichter2020}; flow-battery extensions calculate concentration, current and potential distributions across porous cells \\citep{Chakrabarti2020,AparicioMauricio2023}.

minimal_claim: Models of other solid-product electrodes show that a growing phase feeds back on reaction area and transport.
source_locator: LiS_ROM_JES2025 pp.2-4, Eq.13 and porosity/reaction-area formulation; ZnO_EEM2024 pp.1-4, Figs.1-4; Li2S_EcoMat2024 pp.1-2 and 5-8, Figs.1,3-4
evidence_excerpt_le25_words: LiS model: 'effect of porosity variation on the active reaction area'.
assessment: Only LiS_ROM is a model. ZnO_EEM and Li2S_EcoMat are experimental/in-situ studies. All support solid-phase feedback, but the source type is misstated.
safer_wording: Studies of other solid-product electrodes, including reduced-order modeling and operando characterization, show how precipitation can alter reaction area and transport \\citep{LiS_ROM_JES2025,ZnO_EEM2024,Li2S_EcoMat2024}.

minimal_claim: Periodic DFT calculations used CP2K/Quickstep.
source_locator: Kuhne2020CP2K JCP 152, 194103, title/overview; VandeVondele2005 pp.103-128, abstract and implementation sections
evidence_excerpt_le25_words: Quickstep is part of the CP2K program package.
assessment: Both primary software/method papers are correctly matched to CP2K and its Quickstep GPW implementation.
safer_wording: Retain; add the actual CP2K version/build in the final Methods or SI.

minimal_claim: The exchange-correlation functional was PBE.
source_locator: Perdew et al., Phys. Rev. Lett. 77, 3865-3868, title and derivation
evidence_excerpt_le25_words: Generalized Gradient Approximation Made Simple.
assessment: This is the canonical primary citation for PBE.
safer_wording: Retain.

minimal_claim: Dispersion was treated with D3(BJ).
source_locator: Grimme2010 JCP 132, 154104, D3 parametrization; Grimme2011 JCC 32, 1456-1465, BJ-damping comparison
evidence_excerpt_le25_words: The same DFT-D3 scheme ... Becke and Johnson (BJ-damping).
assessment: The paired citations correctly distinguish the D3 scheme from the BJ damping form.
safer_wording: Retain.

minimal_claim: DZVP-MOLOPT-SR-GTH basis sets were used.
source_locator: VandeVondele and Hutter, JCP 127, 114105, basis-set construction; CP2K BASIS_MOLOPT documentation for the SR alias
evidence_excerpt_le25_words: SR indicates short-range MOLOPT variants.
assessment: The paper is the correct primary basis-set citation; the exact SR-GTH label is a CP2K library identifier.
safer_wording: Retain; record the basis-set file name and element-specific aliases in SI/source data.

minimal_claim: GTH pseudopotentials were used.
source_locator: Goedecker1996 PRB 54, 1703-1710; Hartwigsen1998 PRB 58, 3641-3662; Krack2005 TCA 114, 145-152
evidence_excerpt_le25_words: Krack: parameter sets include Perdew-Burke-Ernzerhof (PBE).
assessment: The canonical GTH and PBE-optimized parameter papers are correctly cited.
safer_wording: Retain; list the exact q-valence potential names in SI/source data.

minimal_claim: Counterpoise-corrected single-point energies were used for site ordering.
source_locator: Boys and Bernardi, Mol. Phys. 19, 553-566, counterpoise procedure
evidence_excerpt_le25_words: procedures with reduced errors
assessment: This is the original counterpoise citation and supports the named correction method.
safer_wording: Retain; state whether geometry optimization was uncorrected and counterpoise applied only at single points, as already drafted.

minimal_claim: Classical MD used GROMACS.
source_locator: Abraham et al., SoftwareX 1-2, 19-25, software publication
evidence_excerpt_le25_words: GROMACS: High performance molecular simulations.
assessment: Correct primary software citation.
safer_wording: Retain; add actual GROMACS version/build in SI.

minimal_claim: The baseline water model was SPC/E.
source_locator: Berendsen et al., J. Phys. Chem. 91, 6269-6271, model definition
evidence_excerpt_le25_words: The missing term in effective pair potentials.
assessment: Canonical SPC/E citation, despite the non-obvious title.
safer_wording: Retain.

minimal_claim: Halide parameters followed the Joung-Cheatham family.
source_locator: Joung and Cheatham, JPCB 112, 9020-9041, parameterization and tables for TIP3P, TIP4P-Ew and SPC/E
evidence_excerpt_le25_words: parameters ... targeted for ... SPC/E
assessment: The source explicitly includes monovalent halides and SPC/E-compatible parameter sets.
safer_wording: Retain; SI must identify the exact Br- and I- row/parameter set used.

minimal_claim: Initial MD configurations were prepared with Packmol.
source_locator: Martinez et al., JCC 30, 2157-2164, abstract and PACKMOL usage section
evidence_excerpt_le25_words: building initial configurations for molecular dynamics simulations
assessment: Exact software-purpose match.
safer_wording: Retain.

minimal_claim: Electronic-continuum charge scaling was compared with formal ionic charges.
source_locator: Leontyev and Stuchebrukhov, JCP 130, 085102, abstract, model definition and conclusions
evidence_excerpt_le25_words: all atomic charges are scaled to reflect the screening effect
assessment: The source supports the MDEC/ECC charge-scaling concept. It does not validate the project's I3-/I2Br- analogue parameters or the resulting diffusivities.
safer_wording: Retain the citation and the existing wording 'project-specific analogue parameterizations'; ensure parameters and scaling factor are released.

minimal_claim: Confined iodine can remain reversible in one carbon host whereas a dense iodine film raises interfacial resistance in another.
source_locator: SolidI2 p.6 Fig.4a-c; Jang p.6385 abstract and Figs.3-6
evidence_excerpt_le25_words: Two primary papers report opposite host-specific outcomes.
assessment: The contrast is directly supported and the next sentence correctly prohibits morphology transfer to the present felt.
safer_wording: Retain both the contrast and the non-transfer sentence.

minimal_claim: Condition-specific iodine-dissolution measurements give current scales and show that solvent environment changes iodine dissolution/clearance.
source_locator: Zhao2022 pp.14090-14096, Figs.2-5; UV-vis particle-dissolution model, CA, and flow-cell tests
evidence_excerpt_le25_words: 7.5 vol% acetonitrile increases the rate constant by an order of magnitude
assessment: Direct support: UV-vis and CA quantify dissolution kinetics; ACN changes the rate constant and stable charging range. Conditions differ from the present 4 M NH4Br system.
safer_wording: Published particle-dissolution and chronoamperometric measurements show that solvent environment can change iodine dissolution kinetics and condition-specific current scales \\citep{Zhao2022}.

minimal_claim: UV-visible approaches have quantified iodine species in related systems.
source_locator: ZnNC_AEL2025 p.1735, Fig.2c-f and Fig.S10; WangNatCommun2025PSI pp.5-6, Fig.3a and Supplementary Figs.11-12
evidence_excerpt_le25_words: Wang: calibration curve between absorption intensity and concentration.
assessment: ZnNC quantifies I-/I3- adsorption and tracks dissolved I3-; Wang calibrates I3- absorbance-concentration and performs in-situ UV-vis in a polysulfide-iodide RFB. Neither measures local free I2 or accessible area.
safer_wording: UV-visible protocols have quantified dissolved I3- or I-/I3- adsorption in related iodine cells \\citep{ZnNC_AEL2025,WangNatCommun2025PSI}.

minimal_claim: Impedance can follow iodine-associated interfacial resistance.
source_locator: Jang2021 pp.6388-6391, Figs.3-6 and EIS protocol
evidence_excerpt_le25_words: Rct increased 9-fold to 147.4 ohm cm2.
assessment: Direct EIS/chronoamperometry support for an iodine-film-associated charge-transfer resistance, in Jang's electrolyte and current regime.
safer_wording: Retain with the Jang-system boundary.

minimal_claim: COMSOL Multiphysics 6.3 solves the coupled tertiary-current, porous-flow and transport model.
source_locator: No COMSOL/software entry occurs in refs.bib; no module/build citation is attached in the draft
evidence_excerpt_le25_words: No cited source.
assessment: The statement may be true as project provenance, but the paper currently gives neither a software citation nor full version/build/module identification. General porous-electrode citations do not substitute for software provenance.
safer_wording: COMSOL Multiphysics 6.3 [exact build; Electrochemistry/CFD modules], COMSOL AB, Stockholm, Sweden, was used ...; add a software reference or complete vendor citation and archive the model report.

minimal_claim: I3-, I2Br- and aqueous I2 rapidly interconvert and are partitioned by beta=1+K_I c_I-+K_Br c_Br-.
source_locator: Palmer1984 p.673 Eq.1 and Tables III-V support I2/I-/I3- equilibrium; Park2021 abstract/results and Weng2017 p.1 support I2Br- formation; neither Palmer nor the current draft establishes a measured common rapid kinetic timescale
evidence_excerpt_le25_words: Park: estimated the stability constant of I2Br- and fractional diagrams.
assessment: The draft treats local equilibrium as an established rapid kinetic fact and gives no source for K_I or K_Br at first use. Palmer cannot support K_Br. The constants are conditional cross-system priors, not present-electrolyte measurements.
safer_wording: Write 'treated as a locally equilibrated lumped pool' and cite Palmer1984 for I3- plus Park2021/Weng2017 for I2Br-. State K_I and K_Br values and classify both as conditional literature priors in SI.

minimal_claim: The effective iodine solubility includes a specified salting correction.
source_locator: Ramette1965 Table I/K tables give the dilute-water reference; Palmer1984 pp.679-681 Table V gives NaClO4 ionic-strength dependence, not direct 1 M ZnI2 + 4 M NH4Br solubility
evidence_excerpt_le25_words: R576: Palmer medium is a cross-salt thermodynamic anchor, not present-electrolyte data.
assessment: The draft omits the values and provenance. Existing SI classifies c_sat=1.33 mol m-3 as a dilute-water reference and gamma_salt=3 as a bounded cross-salt prior; that limitation must survive R582.
safer_wording: At first use cite Ramette1965 for intrinsic solubility and Palmer1984 only as a bounded cross-salt salting prior; state that gamma_salt is not measured in the present electrolyte and retain its sensitivity range.

minimal_claim: Project-specific I3-/I2Br- analogue force fields and one trajectory per composition provide a mobility prior.
source_locator: The standard citations cover GROMACS, SPC/E, JC halides, Packmol and ECC, but no external source validates the project-specific oxidized-iodine analogues
evidence_excerpt_le25_words: No external validation located.
assessment: The draft is appropriately cautious, but the standard method papers cannot be cited as validation of the analogue force fields or diffusivity values.
safer_wording: Keep 'project-specific analogue parameterizations' and 'mobility prior'; release topology/parameter files, scaling factor, convergence/block analysis and avoid 'validated'.
